name: Harness Phase 1 — Prompt Distribution
objective:
Ship the planner and executor system prompts as static docs that any runtime can copy-paste, PLUS a `relayhq prompt` CLI command that outputs them dynamically with the current server URL injected. This makes RelayHQ self-describing: a new runtime can run `relayhq prompt executor --task <id>` and get a ready-to-paste context block with the server URL, task details, and protocol already filled in. Static files are the source of truth; the CLI command injects dynamic values at runtime.

CRITICAL design note: The two prompts encode the planner/executor LANE SPLIT. Planner prompt: read/plan/create tasks only, never claim. Executor prompt: claim first, heartbeat, done — never create new tasks. This prevents a Claude acting as planner from accidentally claiming execution locks, or an executor from spawning off-plan subtasks.
exit_criteria: docs/prompts/planner.md exists and encodes the PLANNER lane contract (read context → check open tasks → propose → create via API → never claim).
docs/prompts/executor.md exists and encodes the EXECUTOR lane contract (claim first → get bootstrap → heartbeat → request-approval → done → never create tasks).
Both prompts include the correct API endpoints and CLI commands with {BASE_URL} placeholder.
CLI `relayhq prompt planner` prints planner.md with BASE_URL filled from env/flag.
CLI `relayhq prompt executor --task <taskId>` fetches bootstrap and prints executor.md with task context filled in.
No test runner needed for prompt files — exit criteria is human-readable quality check.
